calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2025 Continued To Sep-30-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2025 Continued To Dec-31-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Nov-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Matthew Kita, Esq. 214-699-1863, Elise Sanguinetti, Esq. 510-629-4877 ARias Sanguinetti Wang & Team for Plaintiff.
Karen Prodromo, Esq. 925-357-3456 Lewis Brisbois for Defendant. (In-person)

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received from Plaintiff.

ARGUED. Ms. Prodromo submits to the TR. Mr. Kita presents arguments; 2 motions arose from one incident; The Tharp case and the Marshall case, the Marshall case was continued to December; Gov. Newsom has signed a new bill re ADR, requests to continue due to legislative development. The Court denies the request to continue due to lack of diligence. Ms. Prodromo responds; whether arbitration agreement exists, delegation claims, issues to be addressed by the arbitrator. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Defendant Airbnb's Motion to Compel Arbitration is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
